title: Lab: Repair a Volunteer Profile before Publication
summary: Lab: Repair a Volunteer Profile before Publication uses an original volunteer profile incident scenario to retrieve earlier HTML skills, add a bounded artifact layer, and verify transfer with source and browser evidence.
objectives: Retrieve html elements nesting, html attributes without step-by-step imitation.
Produce and verify a real volunteer profile incident artifact or evidence report.
Preserve earlier valid requirements while diagnosing changed content and constraints.

starterFiles.html: <!-- Build the public information artifact here. -->

steps.title: source-to-DOM forensic comparison inspect 1 · lab
steps.instruction: Checkpoint 1: Repair a volunteer profile whose malformed nesting, duplicate identifiers, and misplaced contact link create different source, DOM, and announced relationships. Which conclusion is supported by validator output, parsed DOM relationships, unique identifiers, keyboard order, and the announced name-photo-contact structure? Work context: volunteer profile incident.
steps.why: Lab: Repair a Volunteer Profile before Publication requires this decision because each decision must preserve document meaning, user control, portability, and inspectable evidence. This is checkpoint 1 in the volunteer profile incident.

content.title: volunteer profile incident · evidence decision
content.text: Commit before feedback, then connect the selected claim to the volunteer profile incident evidence.

hints: Lab: Repair a Volunteer Profile before Publication: first isolate the evidence required by “source-to-DOM forensic comparison inspect 1.”
Lab: Repair a Volunteer Profile before Publication: compare the current artifact with the named inspect goal for step 1.
Lab: Repair a Volunteer Profile before Publication: use the smallest defensible change or claim that satisfies “source-to-DOM forensic comparison inspect 1.”

options.text: Volunteer profile incident case 1: indenting a paragraph beneath main makes it a child even if the tags close out of order. Accept the visible source-to-DOM forensic comparison result as proof.

Volunteer profile incident case 1: parent-child relationships come from correctly nested tags; indentation should then mirror that structure. Apply it to the source-to-DOM forensic comparison changed case.

Volunteer profile incident case 1: a browser screenshot proves the source tree is correctly nested. Skip validator output, parsed DOM relationships, unique identifiers, keyboard order, and the announced name-photo-contact structure.

steps.title: source-to-DOM forensic comparison sequence 2 · lab
steps.instruction: Checkpoint 2: Repair a volunteer profile whose malformed nesting, duplicate identifiers, and misplaced contact link create different source, DOM, and announced relationships. Order the evidence so each observation justifies the next decision. Work context: volunteer profile incident.
steps.why: Lab: Repair a Volunteer Profile before Publication depends on causal ordering because each decision must preserve document meaning, user control, portability, and inspectable evidence. This is checkpoint 2 in the volunteer profile incident.

content.text: Reconstruct the volunteer profile incident sequence by dependency, not by how familiar each term looks.

hints: Lab: Repair a Volunteer Profile before Publication: first isolate the evidence required by “source-to-DOM forensic comparison sequence 2.”

options.text: volunteer profile incident phase 2.1: identify the behavior or information the element needs
volunteer profile incident phase 2.2: confirm the attribute is valid for that element
volunteer profile incident phase 2.3: use the required value syntax or boolean presence
volunteer profile incident phase 2.4: inspect the resulting DOM property and behavior

options.text: Volunteer profile incident case 8: every attribute becomes more effective when given the text value true. Accept the visible source-to-DOM forensic comparison result as proof.
Volunteer profile incident case 8: attribute syntax and values must follow the contract defined for that element and attribute. Apply it to the source-to-DOM forensic comparison changed case.

Volunteer profile incident case 8: boolean attributes are enabled only when written with the numeric value 1. Skip validator output, parsed DOM relationships, unique identifiers, keyboard order, and the announced name-photo-contact structure.
